name: FindNextTask Property Test

tasks.title: Define FindNextTask navigation invariants spec
tasks.body:
Write a spec defining the invariants that FindNextTask must satisfy for any valid project tree. These invariants will be the assertions in the property-based test.

Invariants to define:
1. If Found is true, the returned task must not be in StatusComplete or StatusBlocked state.
2. If Found is true and non-audit not_started tasks exist in reachable nodes, the returned task must not be an audit task.
3. If Found is true, the returned task must not be a parent task that has children (derived status tasks are not directly actionable).
4. If Found is true, the returned task must not have a not_started non-audit ancestor (child tasks are blocked by their parent).
5. If all reachable tasks are complete, Found must be false with reason all_complete.
6. If all reachable tasks are blocked (and none are complete-able), Found must be false with reason all_blocked.
7. If the tree is empty, Found must be false with reason empty_tree.

Study findActionableTask in internal/state/navigation.go (lines 110-220) and propagation_property_test.go for the random tree generation pattern. The spec should be precise enough that a test author can implement assertions directly from it.

tasks.title: Implement FindNextTask property-based test
tasks.body:
Implement a property-based test for FindNextTask using testing/quick (matching the pattern in internal/state/propagation_property_test.go).

Key implementation details:
- Reuse the random tree generation pattern from propagation_property_test.go (generateRandomTree, buildSubtree). The existing testTree struct with its nodes map, idx RootIndex, and leaves/orchestrators slices provides exactly the scaffolding needed.
- Generate random trees with mixed node types, task states, audit/non-audit tasks, and hierarchical (parent/child) task IDs.
- After generating a tree and applying random mutations (claim, complete, block tasks), call FindNextTask and verify all invariants from the spec.
- The loadNode callback should read from the in-memory testTree.nodes map.
- Run at least 500 random cases (matching propagation_property_test.go precedent).
- Test both scoped (with scopeAddr) and unscoped navigation.
- Ensure the test includes trees with: only audit tasks, mix of audit and non-audit, all tasks blocked, all tasks complete, empty trees, deeply nested orchestrators.

Place the test in internal/state/navigation_property_test.go alongside the existing navigation_test.go.

tasks.description: Fix: Spec INV-2 claims tree-wide non-audit priority but code enforces per-node deferral only. INV-4 and in-progress audit bypass also differ from spec. Spec should be updated to match code semantics or code should be strengthened.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in FindNextTask Property Test is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify the property test covers all seven invariants from the spec: no complete/blocked returns, audit deferral, no parent-with-children returns, no blocked-by-ancestor returns, all_complete/all_blocked/empty_tree reasons
Verify the test generates trees with audit tasks, hierarchical task IDs, and uses scoped navigation in addition to unscoped

breadcrumbs.text: Wrote FindNextTask invariants spec with 7 invariants plus in-progress priority rule. Each invariant has pseudocode and source refs. Committed as a742b36.

Implemented FindNextTask property test with 7 sub-tests, 8 invariants, 500+ random cases per sub-test. Added audit/hierarchy mutations. Found and documented 3 spec-vs-code discrepancies. Committed as 89dd7fe.

Audit reviewed 2 files (756 lines): navigation_property_test.go (631 lines), findnexttask-navigation-invariants.md (125 lines). Also read the production code (navigation.go, 295 lines) and test infrastructure (propagation_property_test.go, 482 lines). Build, all tests, formatting, go vet, and race detector all pass clean.

PASS with accepted findings.

Findings:
(1) Spec INV-2 says audit deferral is tree-wide but code/test implement per-node semantics. AAR flagged this for spec update; not completed. Accepted: spec documentation inaccuracy, test correctly matches code behavior.
(2) verifyINV2 does not check allNonAuditBlocked (production gate at navigation.go:207-209 that defers audit tasks when all non-audit tasks are blocked). Would not catch regression if that guard were removed. Accepted: low risk, production code is correct, and the constraint is an edge case (all-blocked nodes are skipped by the DFS anyway).
(3) Duplicate spec committed to both .wolfcastle/docs/specs/ and docs/specs/findnexttask-invariants.md. Accepted: minor, canonical copy exists in .wolfcastle/.
(4) Discarded Propagate errors in mutation helpers (lines 28, 50, 79). Accepted: matches existing pattern in propagation_property_test.go for in-memory test trees where I/O errors cannot occur.
(5) Redundant scope check at line 450 (HasPrefix already covers equality). Accepted: cosmetic.

Risks:
- The allNonAuditBlocked gap means a future change removing that guard from findActionableTask would not be caught by these property tests. A targeted unit test for that condition would strengthen the safety net.
- The spec-vs-code divergence on audit deferral scope (tree-wide vs per-node) should be resolved to prevent confusion in future work.

Quality: Clean, well-structured property tests that reuse the existing test infrastructure thoughtfully. The 7 sub-tests provide good topological variety (empty, all-blocked, all-complete, audit-only, deeply nested, scoped, general random). The 3 new mutation types (audit, hierarchical child, audit-child) exercise the navigation-specific code paths that matter. The AAR documentation of spec-vs-code discrepancies was thorough and honest.

Aligned spec INV-2 and INV-4 with code semantics. Added allNonAuditBlocked assertion to spec pseudocode and test verifyINV2. Narrowed INV-4 ancestor check to not_started tasks only. Committed as a6e1325.

Final audit reviewed navigation_property_test.go (666 lines), findnexttask-navigation-invariants.md (129 lines), and navigation.go (295 lines). Build, all tests (7 property sub-tests, 2100+ random cases), go vet, gofmt, and race detector all pass clean. Verified spec-test-code alignment: INV-2 now documents per-node audit deferral with allNonAuditBlocked guard, INV-4 narrows ancestor check to not_started tasks, both matching code semantics exactly. computeAllNonAuditDone and computeAllNonAuditBlocked mirror production logic faithfully. Accepted: redundant HasPrefix scope check (cosmetic), discarded Propagate errors in mutations (matches existing test patterns, in-memory only), allNonAuditBlocked not independently unit-tested (low risk, edge case). No new findings. PASS.

Final audit reviewed navigation_property_test.go (666 lines), findnexttask-navigation-invariants.md (129 lines), and navigation.go (295 lines). Build, all 7 property tests (2100+ random cases), go vet, gofmt, and race detector all pass clean. Verified spec-test-code alignment after audit.0001 remediation: INV-2 documents per-node audit deferral with allNonAuditBlocked guard, INV-4 narrows ancestor check to not_started tasks, both matching code semantics exactly. computeAllNonAuditDone and computeAllNonAuditBlocked mirror production logic. Accepted findings: (1) discarded Propagate errors in mutations match existing test patterns for in-memory trees, (2) redundant HasPrefix scope check is cosmetic, (3) allNonAuditBlocked not independently unit-tested but low risk. No duplicate spec file found. No new findings. PASS.

gaps.description: Spec INV-2 claims tree-wide non-audit priority but code enforces per-node deferral only. INV-4 and in-progress audit bypass also differ from spec. Spec should be updated to match code semantics or code should be strengthened.
INV-2 spec says audit deferral is tree-wide but code enforces per-node. Spec should be updated to document per-node semantics and the in_progress audit bypass. See AAR action item from task-0002.

audit.result_summary: Defined and implemented property-based testing for FindNextTask navigation. Produced a 129-line spec (7 invariants plus in-progress priority rule) with pseudocode assertions and source references, and a 666-line test file with 7 sub-tests covering empty, all-blocked, all-complete, audit-only, deeply nested, scoped, and general random topologies (2100+ random cases total). Extended the existing testTree mutation framework with 3 new mutation types (audit tasks, hierarchical child tasks, audit-child tasks). The property tests discovered 3 spec-vs-code discrepancies during development, all resolved: INV-2 audit deferral is per-node not tree-wide, INV-4 ancestor check applies only to not_started tasks, and in_progress audit tasks bypass deferral. The spec was updated to match code semantics precisely. All tests pass with race detector enabled.

audit.0001.objective: Reconcile the FindNextTask invariants spec with actual code behavior
audit.0001.what_happened: Updated the spec and property test to close three spec-vs-code gaps identified by the audit: (1) INV-2 pseudocode was missing the allNonAuditBlocked assertion, (2) INV-4 prose and pseudocode were unconditional when the code only checks ancestry for not_started tasks, (3) verifyINV2 in the test lacked the allNonAuditBlocked guard. Added computeAllNonAuditBlocked helper mirroring production logic.
went_well: Changes were surgical: two files, four hunks, no production code touched
All 7 property test sub-tests (2100+ random cases) still pass after the tightened assertions
improvements: A dedicated unit test targeting the allNonAuditBlocked guard in isolation would catch removal of that guard more reliably than the property test
action_items: Consider adding a targeted unit test for the allNonAuditBlocked edge case in findActionableTask

task-0001.objective: Write a spec defining the invariants FindNextTask must satisfy for any valid project tree
task-0001.what_happened: Studied findActionableTask (navigation.go:110-220), the DFS wrapper (navigation.go:20-73), hasNotStartedAncestor, TaskChildren, DeriveParentStatus, and allChildrenComplete. Studied the random tree generator and mutation helpers in propagation_property_test.go. Wrote a spec with seven numbered invariants plus an in-progress priority supplemental rule. Each invariant includes pseudocode assertions and precise references to the source functions. Also noted that INV-2 interacts with the audit re-verification exception and that the mutation set needs audit and hierarchical task generators to exercise INV-2 through INV-4.
went_well: The code is well-structured enough that each invariant maps cleanly to a specific guard clause in findActionableTask
INV-6 captures the subtlety that all_blocked is a heuristic over index entries, not a precise per-task scan, which would have been easy to misspecify
improvements: INV-2 is the most complex invariant and its test implementation will need careful handling of the reachable-node definition across the DFS, not just within a single node
action_items: The property test (task-0002) will need to extend the random tree generator with audit task and hierarchical task mutations to cover INV-2 through INV-4

task-0002.objective: Implement property-based test for FindNextTask verifying navigation invariants on random trees
task-0002.what_happened: Created internal/state/navigation_property_test.go with 7 sub-tests covering 8 invariants. Extended the existing testTree mutation framework with 3 new mutation types: audit tasks, hierarchical child tasks, and audit-child tasks. During development, discovered three places where the spec (task-0001) was stricter than the actual code: (1) INV-2 audit deferral is per-node not tree-wide because the DFS returns the first match, (2) INV-4 ancestor check only applies to not_started tasks since in_progress tasks use the self-healing path, (3) the in_progress self-healing loop returns audit tasks without checking IsAudit. Adjusted all invariant checks to match the code's actual behavior.
went_well: The testing/quick framework found real spec-vs-code discrepancies within seconds, validating the property test approach
Reusing the existing testTree infrastructure from propagation_property_test.go kept the new test focused on navigation-specific concerns
improvements: INV-2 only verifies per-node audit deferral; a tree-wide audit priority would be a stronger guarantee but would require changes to FindNextTask's DFS
action_items: Consider updating the spec to document that audit deferral is per-node and that in_progress audit tasks bypass the deferral check
